scenario: Iris Insurance buckets 5200 claims per day by ISO calendar week in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL expression on a business rule input must derive the ISO week number from the claim date, using the documented week of year function applied to the value 2019-09-17 so the output drives downstream routing.
question: What does the FEEL week of year function return for the input date 2019-09-17?

options.text: It returns the integer 38 because the temporal functions reference defines the Gregorian week number within the year following the ISO 8601 calendar rule
It returns the string Week 38 of 2019 because the function formats the ISO week number alongside the year using a fixed text template before returning it
It returns the integer 37 because the function counts the number of full weeks that have ended in the calendar year before the input date value
It returns the integer 260 because the function returns the ordinal day of the year, from which the caller divides by 7 to derive the week of year

a.text: Correct. Week of year returns a number that follows the ISO 8601 calendar rule, so the call on 2019-09-17 yields 38, as shown in the documented temporal functions example.

b.text: Incorrect. The function returns a number rather than a formatted string; any text template must be assembled by the caller from the numeric result.

c.text: Incorrect. The function returns the ISO week containing the input date, not the count of full weeks already ended before that date.

d.text: Incorrect. The function returns the ISO week number directly rather than the ordinal day of the year, which would require a different temporal function.

explanation: FEEL week of year takes a date or date and time value and returns the Gregorian ISO 8601 number of the week within the year, returning 38 for 2019-09-17.
